engineGuide.h2: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mercedes-Benz AMG GT across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol (Base Tune)
entries.history: The M178 is AMG's first hand-built dry-sump V8, designed exclusively for the GT coupe and roadster range with a hot-vee layout for compact packaging.
commonFailures: Timing chain guide wear from 50,000 miles (MB Service Campaign 2021-03B)
Turbocharger actuator moisture ingress causing limp mode
Coolant crossover pipe cracking under thermal load
entries.cta: Get quotes for M178 Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol (Performance Tune)
entries.history: The LS1 variant introduced revised turbos and ECU calibration for the GT S, delivering 510hp while maintaining the M178's signature cross-plane crank growl.
commonFailures: Oil starvation to turbo bearings during cold starts (MB Technical Bulletin LI-21-08)
Valve stem seal failure causing excess oil consumption (MB Recall 22V-112, 2014-2017 models)
Intercooler leak in hot-vee configuration
entries.cta: Get quotes for M178 LS1 Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol (High-Performance Tune)
entries.history: The LS2 powers the track-focused GT R and GT R Pro, featuring revised turbos, upgraded oiling, and unique engine mapping for 585hp.
compatibleVariants: GT R, GT R Pro, GT R Roadster
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing wear identical to base M178
EGR cooler casting flaw causing coolant loss
Oil pump failure on high-mileage track-use examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for M178 LS2 Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol (Black Series Flat-Plane Crank)
entries.history: The ultimate M178 features a flat-plane crankshaft-a first for a series-production AMG V8-unleashing 730hp from the 4.0-litre unit in the limited-run Black Series.
commonFailures: Wiring harness damage to speed sensors (recall on 1,700+ units, March 2022)
Flat-plane crank specific oiling system sensitivity
High-load cooling system demands
entries.cta: Get quotes for M178 LS3 Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol (Second Generation)
entries.history: The second-generation C192 GT adopted the M177 engine with a wet-sump architecture, shared with the broader AMG V8 range but retaining AMG's "One Man, One Engine" assembly.
commonFailures: ISG (Integrated Starter-Generator) overheating in mild-hybrid system

entries.code: Inherited EGR cooler cracking from M17
entries.title: 8 lineage
entries.cta: Get quotes for M177 Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol (High-Output)
entries.history: The LS2 designation on the second-generation M177 brings enhanced oiling and revised turbos to the GT 63, pushing output beyond 600hp in the range-topping variants.
commonFailures: Oil vapour separator (OVS) rear main seal leakage in early LS2 builds
Turbocharger actuator calibration drift
Cylinder head carbon build-up on direct-injection variants
entries.cta: Get quotes for M177 LS2 Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol Mild Hybrid
entries.history: The M256 is Mercedes' modular straight-six, featuring a 48V integrated starter-generator (ISG) and electric auxiliary components-no serpentine belt-delivering V8 power with four-cylinder consumption.
commonFailures: Electric coolant pump failure in 48V system
ISG module overheating (83-vehicle recall on 2023-2024 models)
Low‑temperature coolant circuit thermostat failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for M256 Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol Turbo
entries.history: Dubbed the "world's most powerful four-cylinder," the hand-built M139 delivers over 400hp from 2.0 litres-but AMG fans rejected replacing the V8 with a four-pot in the GT 43.
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump seizure (A0010704601) after 70,000 miles
Turbocharger bearing wear at high boost pressures
Integrated starter-generator (ISG) overheating in 48V system
entries.cta: Get quotes for M139 Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine replacement

entries.title: 5.5L Petrol Turbo
entries.history: Before the M178, early C190 GT models briefly used the 5.5-litre M157 from the previous AMG generation-a thunderous V8 ultimately replaced by the smaller, more efficient M178.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear across all power outputs (MB LI-21-08)
Cylinder head bolt stretching
Turbocharger oil feed line coking
entries.cta: Get quotes for M157 Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The M178 V8 is a masterpiece of performance engineering, but even hand-built AMG units have known weak points. Timing chain stretch and turbo failures dominate replacement demand across C190 and R190 GT models. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Below we break down the four most common AMG GT engine problems, what genuine repair costs look like, and when replacement is the smarter financial decision.

problems.group: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Timing Chain Stretch
problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Timing Chain Stretch - Why M178 V8 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT, GT S, GT C, GT R, GT Roadster (2015-2021, M178, M178 LS1, M178 LS2)
problems.rootCause: The single-row timing chain fitted to early M178 engines elongates under high thermal load, particularly on cars with frequent track use or aggressive downshifting. Chain slack allows the camshaft sprockets to jump timing, leading to valve-to-piston contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides, cam sprockets
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only before chain slip occurs. No warning signs = act early.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace bent valves, lap seats, new gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: For engines with minor valve contact. Adds 40-60k miles typically.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt engine with upgraded chain and guides
repairOptions.longevity: Complete solution with 12-month warranty. Includes uprated double-row chain.

problems.recommendation: On an AMG GT worth £35,000-£80,000, preventative chain replacement is significantly cheaper than waiting for failure. The moment you hear a metallic rattle on cold start from the rear of the engine, act immediately. If the chain has already slipped, replacement costs approach rebuild territory - at which point a fully rebuilt engine with upgraded internals makes better financial sense than repairing damaged heads.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M178 engine prices for your Mercedes-Benz AMG GT

problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Turbocharger Failure - Boost Leak & Actuator Failure
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT, GT S, GT C, GT R (2015-2021, M178 LS1, M178 LS2, M178 LS3)
problems.rootCause: The hot-vee turbo placement exposes actuator electronics to extreme heat cycles, causing internal circuit board failure. Wastegate bushings wear, leading to boost pressure loss and eventual turbo overspin.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace electronic boost actuator only, recalibrate
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught before wastegate damage. 40-60k mile lifespan.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbos, replace bearings, wastegates, actuators
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for cars with worn but not seized turbos. Adds 50k+ miles.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Turbos
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with reinforced turbo seals and upgraded actuators
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended when both turbos have failed and engine has high miles.

problems.recommendation: Turbo actuator failure is often misdiagnosed as boost leak or vacuum issues. Get a diagnostic scan first. If only the actuator has failed, replacement is cost-effective. If wastegate bushings are also worn or turbos have oil seal leaks, a full turbo rebuild is the sensible middle ground. For high-mileage cars (80k+), investing in a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo components protects your GT for years.
problems.cta: Get quotes for M178 turbo repair and replacement

problems.group: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Intercooler Coolant Leak
problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Intercooler Coolant Leak - Hot-V Crossover Pipe Failure
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT, GT S, GT C, GT R (2015-2021, all M178 variants)
problems.rootCause: The plastic coolant crossover pipe mounted between the cylinder banks in the hot-vee degrades from sustained heat exposure. Once cracked, coolant leaks directly onto the hot turbo housings - creating steam, triggering low coolant warnings, and eventually causing overheating.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace plastic pipe with OEM or upgraded aluminium unit, refill coolant
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix if upgraded aluminium pipe used. 1-2 day job.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace crossover pipe, both intercooler bricks, coolant hoses
repairOptions.longevity: For cars with chronic coolant loss or evidence of overheating.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded cooling system components
repairOptions.longevity: Required if engine overheated severely causing head gasket or ring failure.

problems.recommendation: This is the most common coolant-related failure on M178 engines. Do not ignore low coolant warnings - driving with a cracked crossover pipe leads to rapid overheating and catastrophic engine damage. Replace the plastic pipe with an aftermarket aluminium unit as a preventative measure on any GT over 40,000 miles. If caught early, the pipe replacement alone solves the problem for good.
problems.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz AMG GT cooling system repair quotes

problems.group: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Oil Consumption & Valve Stem Seal Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Oil Consumption & Valve Stem Seal Failure
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT, GT S, GT C (2015-2019, M178 & M178 LS1)
problems.rootCause: The original valve stem seals on early M178 engines harden prematurely, allowing oil to seep past the valve guides into the combustion chamber. The result is blue smoke on cold start and after overrun, plus steadily increasing oil consumption (1 litre per 600-1,000 miles).

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all 32 valve stem seals using compressed air to hold valves
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix with upgraded Viton seals. 2-3 day labour-intensive job.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace seals, valve springs, lapping, new head gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: For engines with additional valve train wear or misfires.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded seals and revised valve guides
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage cars (75k+) needing other work alongside seals.

problems.recommendation: Valve stem seal failure does not cause immediate engine death, but excessive oil consumption can foul catalytic converters and O2 sensors (£1,500-£2,500 repair). On a GT worth preserving, specialist seal replacement using upgraded Viton seals is excellent value at around £1,800-£2,800. However, if your car has over 70,000 miles and also shows timing chain wear or turbo issues, a fully rebuilt engine with all upgrades is the more economical long-term decision.
problems.cta: Get quotes for M178 valve stem seal repair

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes-Benz AMG GT
finalCta.paragraph: These four failure points - timing chain stretch, turbo actuator failure, crossover pipe coolant leaks, and valve stem seal degradation - account for over 80% of M178 engine replacements in the UK. The good news is that every failure has a predictable pattern and a cost-effective solution when caught early. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK AMG GT specialists who supply rebuilt engines fitted with upgraded timing chains, reinforced turbo components, aluminium crossover pipes, and Viton valve stem seals - all addressing the original weak points. Every unit comes with a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery or fitting options. Compare prices today and get your AMG GT back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes-Benz AMG GT and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement ever fitted to the AMG GT across all generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years, power outputs and common failure notes.

groups.title: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The M139 four-cylinder engine arrived with the second-generation C192 GT 43 in 2024, marking a controversial departure from the GT's V8 heritage. Despite AMG's claim that it is the "world's most powerful four-cylinder," producing over 400hp from 2.0 litres, enthusiast demand has remained firmly with the V8 options.
commonFailurePoints: turbo bearing wear under sustained boost
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz AMG GT 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The M256 straight-six arrived in the GT 4-Door Coupe (X290) as a mild-hybrid alternative to the V8, offering a balance of refinement and efficiency. With an integrated starter-generator (ISG) and electric auxiliaries eliminating the serpentine belt, this engine delivers V8-like power with improved consumption.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz AMG GT 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The heart of the AMG GT range. The M178 (C190/R190) was AMG's first dedicated dry-sump V8, hand-built under the "One Man, One Engine" principle. The second-generation C192 adopted the wet-sump M177 from 2024. Both share the hot-vee turbo layout and a reputation for prodigious power - and specific failure points that drive replacement demand.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz AMG GT 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT 5.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Before the M178 became standard, a small number of early C190 GT and GT S cars used the M157 5.5-litre biturbo V8 from the previous AMG generation. This thunderous engine delivered immense torque but was quickly phased out in favour of the smaller, more efficient M178.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz AMG GT 5.5L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT 4.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The E Performance badge transforms the GT 63 S into a plug-in hybrid monster, combining the M177 V8 with a rear-axle electric motor and 6.1kWh battery. At 816hp, this is the most powerful road-going AMG GT ever produced, though the added complexity brings unique replacement considerations.
commonFailurePoints: Hybrid system coolant leaks from HV battery thermal management
items.cta: Enquire about Mercedes-Benz AMG GT 4.0L hybrid engine availability

items.title: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Petrol Engines
items.description: The AMG GT is defined by its hand-built V8 petrol engines, though the second-generation C192 introduced a four-cylinder option and a straight-six mild hybrid.
items.descriptor: The AMG GT is defined by its hand-built V8 petrol engines, though the second-generation C192 introduced a four-cylinder option and a straight-six mild hybrid.
families: M178 - The original AMG GT V8, dry-sump, hot-vee, cross-plane crank (GT, GT S, GT C)
M178 LS2 - Track-focused unit for GT R and GT R Pro with upgraded oiling and turbos
M178 LS3 - Flat-plane crank V8 exclusive to the 730hp Black Series
M177 - Wet-sump V8 for second-generation C192 GT 55 and GT 63 models
M256 - 3.0L straight-six mild hybrid for GT 53 4MATIC+ (X290 and C192)
foundIn: C192 Second Generation (2024-present) - M139 (GT 43), M256 (GT 53), M177 (GT 55/63/63 S)
knownFor: Hand-built "One Man, One Engine" assembly with builder's signature on each unit
M178 timing chain stretch as the #1 replacement driver (50k-80k miles)
Hot-vee turbo actuator failures and coolant crossover pipe leaks
Black Series flat-plane crank delivers 730hp - the most powerful AMG V8 ever

items.title: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines
items.description: The second-generation C192 and X290 4-Door Coupe use 48V mild hybrid systems integrated with petrol engines - not full hybrids, but electrified assistance.
items.descriptor: The second-generation C192 and X290 4-Door Coupe use 48V mild hybrid systems integrated with petrol engines - not full hybrids, but electrified assistance.
families: M256 - 3.0L straight-six with Integrated Starter-Generator (ISG) and electric auxiliaries
48-volt integrated starter-generator (ISG) replaces alternator and starter motor
Electric boost fills torque gaps before turbo spools (EQ Boost function)
Regenerative braking recovers energy to a compact lithium-ion battery
importantNotes: MHEV engines require hybrid system inspection during replacement
ISG module overheating documented on 2023-2024 X290 units
No plug-in capability - mild hybrid only, not a PHEV
Battery replacement (when required) adds approximately £800-£1,200 to engine work

items.title: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV) Engines
items.description: The GT 63 S E Performance is the only plug-in hybrid AMG GT, combining the M177 V8 with a rear-axle electric motor for a combined 816hp.
items.descriptor: The GT 63 S E Performance is the only plug-in hybrid AMG GT, combining the M177 V8 with a rear-axle electric motor for a combined 816hp.
families: M177 E20 - 4.0L V8 biturbo with integrated electric motor on rear axle
6.1kWh battery powers a rear-mounted electric motor (150kW / 201hp)
Electric-only range of approximately 8-10 miles at low speeds
Combustion engine and electric motor can operate simultaneously for 816hp peak
importantNotes: High-voltage system requires specialist handling during engine replacement
PHEV engine availability is significantly more limited than standard petrol units

Warranty on hybrid components typically separate from engine warranty

engineYears.h2: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: From the 2015 launch of the C190 coupe to the 2024 second-generation C192, this timeline covers every AMG GT engine change by year - helping you identify your exact unit and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The AMG GT arrives as the spiritual successor to the SLS, powered by AMG's first dedicated dry-sump V8 - the M178.
keyChanges: C190 coupe launched at Paris Motor Show (October 2014)
GT (462hp) and GT S (510hp) variants available from launch
Dry-sump oiling system introduced for track-focused durability
First AMG engine with "hot-vee" turbo layout
mainEngines: M157 - 5.5L petrol (very early GT / GT S units, 550-585hp)
knownFor: Hand-built "One Man, One Engine" assembly signature on every unit
Early M178 units prone to timing chain stretch from 50,000 miles
Dry-sump system requires specialist knowledge for correct oil filling
M157 phased out within 12 months - rare and collectable
years.cta: Get quotes for 2014-2015 Mercedes-Benz AMG GT replacement engines
years.ticker: M178 timing chain stretch • AMG GT engine replacement cost 2015 • M157 AMG GT engine for sale
enquiries: M157 AMG GT engine for sale

years.preview: C190 Expands: GT C & GT R Arrive
years.description: The AMG GT range explodes upward with the GT C (550hp) and the track-focused GT R (585hp), introducing the LS2 engine variant.
keyChanges: GT C coupe introduced (2016) - positioned between GT S and GT R
GT R arrives (2017) with "Green Hell Magno" launch colour
GT C Roadster debuts (2018) - first open-top V8 GT
Revised turbos and oiling system on LS2 units
knownFor: GT R's rear-wheel steering and coil-over suspension set track records
LS2 engines feature upgraded turbo bearings but retain timing chain risk
GT C Roadster remains a high-value collector variant
Cooling system demands increase significantly with higher power outputs
years.cta: Compare 2016-2018 Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine prices
years.ticker: AMG GT R engine replacement • M178 LS2 turbo failure • GT C Roadster engine cost

years.preview: C190 Facelift: GT R Pro & Flat-Plane Black Series
years.description: The C190 receives a facelift with updated styling, while the GT R Pro and the flat-plane crank Black Series become the ultimate M178 expressions.
keyChanges: GT R Pro launched (2019) - aerodynamics and chassis upgrades, same LS2 engine
Black Series debuts (2020) with M178 LS3 flat-plane crank V8
GT R Roadster introduced (2019) - limited-run open-top track car
Infotainment and interior updates across all variants
mainEngines: M178 LS2 - 4.0L petrol (GT R, GT R Pro, GT R Roadster, 585hp)
knownFor: Black Series flat-plane crank is the most powerful series-production AMG V8 ever
LS3 features unique crankshaft, turbos, and oiling system - parts are scarce
GT R Pro limited to approximately 500 units worldwide
Late C190 units benefited from revised timing chain guides
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019-2020 Mercedes-Benz AMG GT replacement engines
years.ticker: AMG GT Black Series engine cost • M178 LS3 replacement availability • GT R Pro engine for sale UK
enquiries: AMG GT Black Series engine cost
GT R Pro engine for sale UK

years.preview: C190 End of Production: Final M178 Cars
years.description: The original C190 coupe and R90 roadster end production in 2021, with remaining stock sold through 2022-2023 as the GT range transitions.
keyChanges: C190 coupe production ends December 2021
GT R discontinued globally after 2021 model year
Black Series production capped at 1,700 units worldwide
Final "Edition" cars feature unique spec and collectable status
knownFor: Final M178 units include all factory revisions for timing chain and seals
Collectors actively seek low-mileage 2021 Black Series and GT R Pro cars
Replacement demand remains high despite low production volumes
Values of late C190 cars have stabilised above early depreciation curves
years.cta: Compare 2021-2023 Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine prices

years.description: The four-door AMG GT (X290) shares the name but introduces M256 straight-six mild hybrid and M177 V8 engines alongside the existing C190 range.
keyChanges: X290 4-Door Coupe launched (2018) on MRA platform
GT 53 introduced with M256 3.0L straight-six mild hybrid
GT 63 / GT 63 S arrive with M177 4.0L V8 (wet-sump)
GT 63 S E Performance PHEV launched (2021, 816hp)
mainEngines: M177 E20 - 4.0L PHEV (GT 63 S E Performance, 816hp combined)
popularModels: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT 63 S E Performance
knownFor: X290 shares no mechanical DNA with C190 - different platform entirely
M256 ISG (integrated starter-generator) failures reported on early units
GT 63 S is the most powerful non-hybrid AMG GT
Four-door body commands different replacement market from coupe
years.cta: Get quotes for X290 Mercedes-Benz AMG GT 4-Door engine replacement
years.ticker: AMG GT 63 engine replacement • M177 wet-sump price • GT 53 M256 hybrid engine cost

years.preview: Present - C192 Second Generation: M139 Four-Cylinder & M177 V8
years.description: The all-new C192 coupe arrives with a controversial four-cylinder entry point and refined V8 options - but no manual gearbox, and dry-sump is gone.
keyChanges: All-new C192 platform (2024) - longer, wider, more tech-focused
GT 43 introduces M139 2.0L four-cylinder (416hp) - first non-V8 GT coupe
GT 55 and GT 63 retain M177 V8 (wet-sump, not dry-sump)
GT 63 S E Performance PHEV carries over from X290 with 816hp
mainEngines: M177 E20 - 4.0L PHEV (GT 63 S E Performance, 816hp)
popularModels: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT 63 S E Performance
knownFor: GT 43's M139 is the most powerful production four-cylinder in any Mercedes
Enthusiasts criticise the loss of V8 from entry-level GT
Wet-sump M177 replaces dry-sump M178 - different failure profile
C192 is significantly heavier than C190 (approx +200kg)

years.ticker: AMG GT 43 engine replacement • M139 2.0L price • C192 GT 63 V8 cost

faq.h2: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt M178 V8 costs £8,000-£16,000 supply-only, rising to £14,000-£22,000 for a Black Series flat-plane crank unit. Prices vary by variant, engine code and whether you need supply & fit.
keyPoints: Base M178 (GT/GT S): £8,000-£15,000 for a rebuilt unit
Performance M178 LS2 (GT R): £11,000-£19,000 supply-only
Black Series M178 LS3: £15,000-£22,000 - limited availability
Supply & fit adds £800-£1,500 to any engine price
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: What are the most common Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine problems?
items.answer: Timing chain stretch (50k-80k miles) is the #1 failure on M178 engines, followed by turbo actuator failure and hot-vee coolant crossover pipe leaks. Valve stem seal hardening also causes oil consumption.
keyPoints: Timing chain stretch leads to valve contact - replace chains preventatively
Turbo actuator electronics fail from heat exposure in the hot-vee
Plastic coolant pipes crack between cylinder banks - upgrade to aluminium
Viton valve stem seals solve oil consumption on early M178 LS1 units
items.cta: Get quotes for M178 timing chain replacement before it's too late

items.question: Is it worth rebuilding an AMG GT engine or should I replace it?
items.answer: On a GT worth £35k-£80k, preventative chain replacement is cost-effective. If the engine has already failed, a fully rebuilt unit with upgraded timing components and seals is usually better value than repairing the damaged original.
keyPoints: Preventative chain replacement at a specialist: £2,400-£3,200
Repairing a slipped chain with head work: £4,500-£6,500
Fully rebuilt M178 with upgrades and warranty: £9,000-£16,000
Matching numbers matter for collectable Black Series and GT R Pro
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine with 12-month warranty

items.question: How long does a Mercedes-Benz AMG GT M178 engine last?
items.answer: Well-maintained M178 engines regularly reach 100k-120k miles. The key is addressing timing chain stretch before it fails - typically around 50k-80k miles. Cars used on track wear chains faster.
keyPoints: Timing chain service interval effectively 50k-80k miles on early cars
Turbo actuator life: 40k-70k miles depending on heat exposure
Valve stem seals typically last 45k-75k miles before hardening
Late C190 units (2019+) have revised chain guides for longer life
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine prices by mileage and condition

items.question: What engine is in my AMG GT? How do I identify the engine code?
items.answer: Check your VIN - digits 4-8 contain the engine code. GT and GT S use M178/M178 LS1, GT R uses M178 LS2, Black Series uses M178 LS3. C192 models (2024+) use M177 (V8) or M139 (four-cylinder).
keyPoints: M178: Base GT (462-476hp) and GT C (550-557hp)
M178 LS1: GT S (510-522hp) and early GT variants
M178 LS2: GT R, GT R Pro (577-585hp)
M178 LS3: Black Series only (720-730hp flat-plane crank)
items.cta: Get your Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine identified by registration

items.question: Can I fit an AMG GT R engine into my standard AMG GT?
items.answer: Yes, the M178 LS2 from a GT R is a direct physical swap into any C190 coupe. However, you'll need the LS2-specific ECU calibration, turbo oiling upgrades, and potentially the GT R's auxiliary oil cooler.
keyPoints: Physical mounting points are identical across all M178 engines
ECU must be reprogrammed with LS2 maps - not a plug-and-play swap
GT R uses uprated turbo bearings and revised oiling passages
Specialist fitting recommended - budget £11,000-£19,000 for the complete job
items.cta: Enquire about performance upgrade engines for your Mercedes-Benz AMG GT

items.question: What warranty comes with a replacement AMG GT engine from EngineMarket?
items.answer: Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Many reconditioned and remanufactured units from specialist AMG rebuilders include 24-month coverage.
keyPoints: Standard warranty: 12 months unlimited mileage on all engine types
Reconditioned/remanufactured units: Often 24 months from AMG specialists
Supply & fit packages: Labour warranty included with fitting (12 months)
Black Series LS3 engines: Limited supply, always confirm warranty terms
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine warranties from trusted UK suppliers

items.question: How long does AMG GT engine replacement take from a UK specialist?
items.answer: Supply-only delivery takes 2-5 working days. Full supply and fit by an AMG specialist typically takes 3-5 days, including removal, installation, fluids, and first-start diagnostics.
keyPoints: Used engine delivery: 2-3 days from UK breaker network
Rebuilt/reconditioned engine delivery: 5-10 days (built to order)
Supply & fit turnaround: 3-5 days at a fixed workshop
Mobile fitting: Available nationwide, add 1-2 days for travel scheduling
items.cta: Get a Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine replacement timeline and quote

items.question: Do you supply upgraded AMG GT engines with reinforced parts?
items.answer: Yes, specialist rebuilders offer upgraded M178 units with double-row timing chains, aluminium coolant crossover pipes, Viton valve stem seals, and reinforced turbo actuators - all addressing the original failure points.
keyPoints: Upgraded timing chain kit: Eliminates stretch risk completely
Aluminium crossover pipe: Permanent fix for hot-vee coolant leaks
Viton valve stem seals: Solve oil consumption on LS1 engines
Upgraded turbo actuators: Heat-resistant electronics for longer life
items.cta: Find upgraded Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engines with failure-proof components

items.question: Do you buy broken or damaged AMG GT engines for core exchange?
items.answer: Yes, many of our specialist suppliers offer core credit for your failed M178, M177 or M139 engine. The credit typically ranges from £1,000-£3,000 depending on the engine code and whether the block is salvageable.
keyPoints: M178 core credit: £1,500-£3,000 for rebuildable units
M139 (four-cylinder) credit: £800-£1,500 - lower demand
Black Series LS3 cores: Valued higher - contact us for a quote
Engine must be complete (no missing turbos or ancillaries)
items.cta: Sell your old Mercedes-Benz AMG GT engine for core credit today
